items.title: Inter interested in Spence - Friday's gossip
items.summary: Inter Milan work on deals for Djed Spence and Cristian Romero, Liverpool lead the race for Kaishu Sano, Arsenal show interest in Ezri Konsa, plus more.

items.title: Players ‘puzzled’ by tactics but Tuchel left himself no other option with his squad picks | Jacob Steinberg
items.summary: The England head coach took a risk by selecting injury-prone players and ignoring creative options such as Foden, Palmer and Gibbs-White When Thomas Tuchel became England’s head coach he spoke repeatedly about wanting his side to adopt a Premier League style. He wanted intensity, pace, full-throttle

items.title: Heartbroken Harry Kane left ‘empty’ and regretting ‘missing piece of puzzle’ as he breaks silence on World Cup KO
items.summary: HARRY KANE has broken his silence on England's World Cup semi-final elimination. items.title: Erling Haaland’s iconic hair tie worn at World Cup and made by remote company sells out following American boom
items.summary: ERLING HAALAND'S iconic hair tie which he sported at the World Cup has sold out after a boom in sales during the tournament. The Norway star striker has become a remarkably popular figure in the US due to his goal scoring heroics and off-field personality. He bagged seven goals in five games to

items.title: Arsène Wenger: US soccer development will depend on ‘consistency’ and ‘education’
items.summary: Former Arsenal boss calls out US pay-to-play model Wenger says academy setups are vital for growth Arsène Wenger, Fifa’s head of global football development, has said that while the US faced numerous structural obstacles to becoming a real soccer power, many of those obstacles are being addressed by

items.title: Highlights: Root's 99 not out guides England to comfortable victory
items.summary: Highlights of the second one-day international match between England and India.

items.title: Root delivers again for England to level ODI series against India
items.summary: Joe Root notched a stunning 99no to deliver again for his country as England secured a thrilling four-wicket win to level the one-day international series against India at Sophia Gardens in Cardiff.

items.title: Tuchel blames English football's 'DNA' for World Cup exit
items.summary: Thomas Tuchel says he is "100 per cent" committed to continuing his role as England boss into the next European Championships in two years, and he has hit back at critics who have questioned his tactics, saying instead it is a problem with English football's "DNA".

items.title: Some England players disappointed at tactics as Tuchel questions team's DNA
items.summary: BBC Sport understands that Thomas Tuchel's defensive tactics in the World Cup semi-final defeat to Argentina are being hotly debated by the England squad.

items.title: Lionel Messi and Argentina will have no answer to Spain… this team is 12 years in the making with a genius of a boss
items.summary: DOESN’T that Cape Verde game feel like a lifetime ago? Spain huffed and puffed for 90 minutes but couldn’t unlock the plucky Africans as they started their World Cup with a shocking 0-0 draw. There didn’t look a great deal to be frightened about that day but it’s fair to say they have grown into...

items.title: Thomas Tuchel facing FA probe into England’s World Cup exit and rollercoaster campaign including TV row with Bellingham
items.summary: THE inquest into England's World Cup exit is set to continue with The FA set to launch a probe into the Three Lions' rollercoaster tournament.
